_meta.note: Adjudicated merge of knighting + suspicion-a/b proposer slices; see ADJUDICATION-s211.md.

edges.quote: Four years later, they anointed him with the seven oils and he recited his knightly vows and Rhaegar Targaryen tapped him on the shoulder and said, ‘Arise, Ser Gregor.’
edges.note: [knighting:K1] Sandor's own recollection names Rhaegar as the dubber, four years after the branding.

edges.quote: The king knighted him before he rode north, in Jon’s memory.
edges.note: [knighting:K2] Barristan Selmy to Ned Stark, confirming Robert dubbed Hugh in Jon Arryn's memory.

edges.quote: King Robert knighted him at Pyke.
edges.note: [knighting:K3] Tyrion, discussing Jacelyn Bywater's record as City Watch candidate.

edges.quote: The Laughing Storm gave an impatient shake of the head. "Go to him, Ser Duncan. I'll give squire Raymun his knighthood."
edges.note: [knighting:K4] On-page dubbing scene at the trial of seven; "the Laughing Storm" = Ser Lyonel Baratheon.

edges.quote: Rolly Duckfield. Any knight can make a knight, and Griff made me.
edges.note: [knighting:K5] Rolly ("Duck") to Tyrion, self-identifying Griff (Jon Connington) as his dubber; corroborated later in same chapter ("Ser Duck says you knighted him").

edges.quote: He had been born during the troubled reign of Aegon the Unlikely, beloved by the common folk, had received his knighthood at his hands.
edges.note: [knighting:K6] Barristan's own POV; "Aegon the Unlikely" = Aegon V Targaryen, at the tourney at King's Landing feast (the wild-boar memory in the boar food node).

edges.quote: Harrold Hardyng. I suppose we must call him Ser Harry now. Bronze Yohn knighted him.
edges.note: [knighting:K7] Littlefinger to Alayne/Sansa, after Harry wins the squires' melee at Runestone.

edges.quote: I was knighted by Ser Morgan Dunstable of Tumbler's Falls, two dozen people saw it
edges.note: [knighting:K8] Ser Glendon (Ball/Flowers) to Dunk, on the road to Whitewalls; corroborated later by Ser Kirby Pimm's account of the same event to Dunk.

edges.quote: He knighted me before he passed, with his own sword.
edges.note: [knighting:K9] Dunk's own account to Plummer at Ashford; reaffirmed to Lady Rohanne Webber in tss and cited again by the Bastard of Harrenhal's node passage. Unwitnessed per the text, but treated as Dunk's canonical dubbing throughout the D&E stories.

edges.quote: The prince had been fostered by Lord Yronwood from a tender age, had served him as a page, then a squire, had even taken knighthood at his hands in preference to the Red Viper's.
edges.note: [knighting:K10] Ser Arys Oakheart POV; "Lord Yronwood" here is Anders Yronwood, the Bloodroyal who fostered Quentyn (confirmed elsewhere in the same chapter file). Different pair than the oberyn-martell node's own knighting fact (Oberyn/Daemon Sand), which is wiki_only.

edges.quote: The Clubfoot was a traitor as well, he decided. How else would Daemon and Rhaenyra have known when King’s Landing was most vulnerable? Someone on the small council had sent word to them...
edges.note: [suspicion-a:S1] Aemond's in-world suspicion (voiced during his Harrenhal reprisal) that Larys Strong fed the blacks intelligence enabling the fall of King's Landing; never confirmed on-page.

edges.quote: the haste with which the castellan had yielded Harrenhal to Daemon Targaryen convinced him the old man was a traitor.
edges.note: [suspicion-a:S2] Aemond suspects Ser Simon Strong of treasonously surrendering Harrenhal to Daemon without a fight; Simon protests innocence and the narrative leaves it genuinely ambiguous (he had no way to hold against a dragon) before Aemond executes him by trial-by-combat verdict, not proof.

edges.quote: the widows of Myr and Tyrosh accused the admiral of sending their fleets to destruction whilst holding back his own
edges.note: [suspicion-a:S3] Myrish/Tyroshi widows accuse Sharako Lohar of deliberately sacrificing allied ships while sparing his own Lysene squadron during the Gullet; presented as accusation/grievance, not confirmed fact.

edges.quote: Ser Morgan Hightower did the deed at the command of his lord brother, some say (and Ser Morgan was seen entering and leaving the High Septon’s privy chambers that night).
edges.note: [suspicion-a:S6] One of several rival theories of the High Septon's death during Maegor's siege of Oldtown; text explicitly frames it as unproven ("some say") alongside competing suspects.

edges.quote: Others point to the Lady Patrice Hightower, Lord Martyn’s maiden aunt and a reputed witch (who did indeed seek an audience with His High Holiness at dusk, though he was alive when she departed).
edges.note: [suspicion-a:S7] A second rival theory in the same passage; text notes she did visit the High Septon that evening but he was alive when she left, leaving her culpability explicitly unresolved.

edges.quote: Ser Morgan Hightower did the deed at the command of his lord brother, some say
edges.note: [suspicion-a:S8] The theory naming Morgan Hightower as the killer also implicates his "lord brother" (Lord Martyn Hightower, Lord of Oldtown) as the one who ordered it — a nested, doubly-unproven suspicion voiced in the same sentence.

edges.quote: Septon’s death at the door of another rumored sorceress, the Dowager Queen Visenya Targaryen.
edges.note: [suspicion-a:S9] The fourth rival theory in the same debated-death passage (matches the node's own Identity summary, "rumor laid his convenient passing at Queen Visenya's door, though nothing was proven"). Quote spans the chapter's own line-wrap.

edges.quote: The old queen was determined that her young half-sister should not live to revel in her downfall, so she had sent Ser Luthor Largent to seize
edges.note: [suspicion-a:S10] The city-wide rumor (which ignites the King's Landing riot) that Rhaenyra ordered Helaena's murder. The very next paragraph in the same chapter states the rumor is almost certainly false and gives Ser Luthor Largent's alibi — SUSPECTED_OF captures the in-world suspicion itself, not a truth claim.

edges.quote: she had sent Ser Luthor Largent to seize
edges.note: [suspicion-a:S11] Same rumor as S10, naming Luthor Largent as the purported hands-on killer. The chapter explicitly refutes this a few lines later with his barracks alibi ("we have abundant proof that Ser Luthor Largent, the purported killer, was eating with three hundred of his gold cloaks at the barracks") — a textbook false-rumor SUSPECTED_OF, not an assertion of fact.

edges.quote: Euron is known to keep wizards and foul sorcerers on that red ship of his. They sent some spell among us, so we could not hear the sea.
edges.note: [suspicion-a:S12] Aeron Damphair's accusation to Victarion that Euron used sorcery to sway/rig the kingsmoot vote in his favor — an unproven in-world accusation (the node's own Foreshadowing section already treats Euron's sorcery as gated-until-TWoW), distinct from the existing euron-greyjoy+death-of-balon-greyjoy pair.

edges.quote: You are accused of sending a hired knife to slay my son Bran in his bed, and of conspiring to murder Lord Jon Arryn, the Hand of the King.
edges.note: [suspicion-b:S1] Catelyn's formal accusation of Tyrion at the Eyrie 'trial' staged by Lysa (encountered while processing lysa-accuses-tyrion-of-poisoning-jon-arryn, a near-empty stub node whose own VICTIM_IN edge already covers Tyrion-as-accused; this edge targets the actual crime event named in that accusation instead).

edges.quote: And all of them had ties to one man: Unwin Peake, Lord of Starpike
edges.note: [suspicion-b:S3] Mushroom's testimony names Peake as the hidden mastermind behind the 42 accused conspirators against the Rogares/Aegon III; 'their involvement was never proven' per the wiki summary too. unwin-peake is already SUSPECTED_OF maidens-day-curse and death-of-jaehaera-targaryen in the global pairs — this is a third, distinct target event (the-treason-trials), not a re-propose. Same underlying Rogare-conspiracy material also touches the lysene-spring and secret-siege nodes; flagged below to avoid a near-duplicate edge landing on those too.

edges.quote: Could he have been the Hand's catspaw? It is more than possible
edges.note: [suspicion-b:S4] Discovered while processing regency-of-aegon-iii (which names both Mervyn Flowers and Tessario the Tiger as wiki-level suspects in Jaehaera's death). unwin-peake is already SUSPECTED_OF this event in the global pairs; Mervyn is a distinct, textually well-supported second suspect (the Kingsguard posted at her door that night, explicitly floated as Peake's catspaw). Declined to also add Tessario — the source text calls that a third-order 'fancy' (Mushroom's guess about who Mervyn might have let in), too hedged to ground cleanly; see flags.

edges.quote: You’ll tell her it is part of my scheme to free Jaime.”
edges.note: [suspicion-b:S5] Retyped from proposed SUSPECTED_OF at orchestrator adjudication: Catelyn's suspicion (acok-catelyn-05) is proven in Tyrion's own POV — the four false guardsmen scheme is his (acok-tyrion-06). Proven agency = COMMANDS_IN (S148 Petyr precedent).

edges.quote: He led the raid on Saltpans, here.
edges.note: [suspicion-b:S6] Ser Hyle Hunt voices the widespread misperception that Sandor led the raid, based on his distinctive helm being seen there. Later disproven in the same node's passages (Elder Brother: Sandor could not have done it, he had buried the man himself) — an honest, textually-attested false suspicion, parallel to the Tyrion/Sansa/Marillion framing pattern elsewhere in this dip.

edges.quote: Lord Randyll is putting it about that they did in hopes of turning the commons against Beric and his brotherhood.
edges.note: [suspicion-b:S7] Randyll Tarly deliberately spreads the rumor blaming Beric Dondarrion's brotherhood for the raid, per the same passage. Faction-level suspect (matches the existing sons-of-the-harpy/poisoned-locusts pattern of a group being the named suspect rather than one member).

edges.quote: the dragon's egg that Lord Peake found amongst Ser Glendon's things was the one he placed there. Let him bring it forth, if he can. Examine it yourself. I'll wager you it's no more than a painted stone.
edges.note: [suspicion-b:S8] Dunk publicly accuses Lord Gormon Peake of planting the 'stolen' dragon egg to frame Glendon Ball and unmask the Whitewalls plot's true instigator. Matches the wiki summary's 'insisted that Lord Peake had plotted everything' / 'painted stone planted by order of Lord Peake' beats.

edges.quote: King Aerys became convinced that his son was conspiring to depose him, that Whent’s tourney was but a ploy to give Rhaegar a pretext for meeting with as many great lords as could be brought together.
edges.note: [suspicion-b:S9] Barristan's retrospective POV confirms the in-world suspicion (voiced to Aerys by Varys) that Rhaegar was the true, secret orchestrator of the tourney as cover for a conspiratorial Great Council — never proven, and distinct from the already-existing rhaegar-targaryen/abduction-of-lyanna pair.

edges.quote: This singer’s killed my lady wife.
edges.note: [suspicion-b:S12] Littlefinger's on-the-spot frame-up of Marillion immediately after pushing Lysa through the Moon Door himself — the wiki summary's 'killing is then blamed on the singer Marillion,' directly book-quoted. Petyr's own guilt is proven in the same passage (he shoves her: '“Only Cat.” He gave her a short, sharp shove.') so this is squarely a false, framed suspicion, not a genuine whodunit — exactly the honest-false-accusation pattern the dip is built to capture.

edges.quote: Lord Velaryon did not attempt to deny his guilt.
edges.note: [orchestrator:+] Orchestrator-added at S211 adjudication of the suspicion-a flag: Corlys confesses before Cregan Stark's tribunal and is declared guilty of murder, regicide, and high treason — proven complicity, so AGENT_IN (role edge), NOT SUSPECTED_OF. Larys Strong's existing tier-2 SUSPECTED_OF stands per the S202/S203 adjudications (he never confirmed; the administering hand is never known).
